coredata.dc:title: Quick Evaluation of the State-of-Health of Spent Lithium-Ion Battery Modules
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Sun, Huiqin
Liu, Wei
Du, Zhichao
Li, Xinzhou
Fu, Zaiguo
Liao, Qiangqiang
coredata.dc:description: A decommissioned battery module with 15 in parallel and 4 series from an electric vehicle was aged at 2 C-rate in the SOC range of 30–80% in the laboratory until its state of health (SOH) value reached about 60%. The module capacity was calibrated at 1/5 C-rate after every 100 or 200 cycles of aging. The probability density function (PDF) method and incremental capacity analysis (ICA) were introduced to establish an SOH model of battery modules against experimental charge and discharge voltage data in the process of capacity calibration. As a new SOH evaluation algorithm, the discrete Fréchet distance was also proposed for the battery module, and the concept of mean Fréchet distance (MFD) was given as a quick evaluation index of the module’s SOH. The results showed that there was a fine negative linear dependence of module SOH on MFD. The discrete Fréchet distance method has better accuracy than ICA and PDF in terms of module SOH evaluation.
coredata.dcterms:subject: retired battery modules
SOH evaluation
incremental capacity analysis
probability density function
discrete Fréchet distance

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Quick Evaluation of the State-of-Health of Spent Lithium-Ion Battery Modules Huiqin Sun, 1 Wei Liu, 2 Zhichao Du, 3 Xinzhou Li, 1 Zaiguo Fu, 1 Qiangqiang Liao, 1 liaoqiangqiang@shiep.edu.cn 1 Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power, Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion, Shanghai University of Electric Power, Shanghai 200090, China Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion Shanghai University of Electric Power Shanghai 200090 China Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power, Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion, Shanghai University of Electric Power, Shanghai 200090, China 2 Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd., Guiyang 550002, China Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd. Guiyang 550002 China Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd., Guiyang 550002, China 3 Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited, Shanghai 201199, China Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited Shanghai 201199 China Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited, Shanghai 201199, China A decommissioned battery module with 15 in parallel and 4 series from an electric vehicle was aged at 2 C-rate in the SOC range of 30â€“80% in the laboratory until its state of health (SOH) value reached about 60%. The module capacity was calibrated at 1/5 C-rate after every 100 or 200 cycles of aging. The probability density function (PDF) method and incremental capacity analysis (ICA) were introduced to establish an SOH model of battery modules against experimental charge and discharge voltage data in the process of capacity calibration. As a new SOH evaluation algorithm, the discrete FrÃ©chet distance was also proposed for the battery module, and the concept of mean FrÃ©chet distance (MFD) was given as a quick evaluation index of the moduleâ€™s SOH. The results showed that there was a fine negative linear dependence of module SOH on MFD. The discrete FrÃ©chet distance method has better accuracy than ICA and PDF in terms of module SOH evaluation. Keywords retired battery modules SOH evaluation incremental capacity analysis probability density function discrete FrÃ©chet distance References 1. Y. Yang, E.G. Okonkwo, G. Huang, S. Xu, W. Sun, Y. He, Energy Storage Mater. 36: (2021) 186-Y. Yang, E. G. Okonkwo, G. Huang, S. Xu, W. Sun and Y. He, Energy Storage Mater., 36 (2021) 186. 2. Y. Zhang, Y. Li, Y. Tao, J. Ye, A. Pan, X. Li, Q. Liao, Z. head.ce:title: Quick Evaluation of the State-of-Health of Spent Lithium-Ion Battery Modules

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power, Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion, Shanghai University of Electric Power, Shanghai 200090, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power
Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Materials Protection and Advanced Materials in Electric Power, Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Electric Energy Conversion, Shanghai University of Electric Power, Shanghai 200090, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd., Guiyang 550002, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd.

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Electric Power Research Institute of Guizhou Power Grid Co., Ltd., Guiyang 550002, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited, Shanghai 201199, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Shanghai Electric Distributed Energy Technology company limited, Shanghai 201199, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A decommissioned battery module with 15 in parallel and 4 series from an electric vehicle was aged at 2 C-rate in the SOC range of 30–80% in the laboratory until its state of health (SOH) value reached about 60%. The module capacity was calibrated at 1/5 C-rate after every 100 or 200 cycles of aging. The probability density function (PDF) method and incremental capacity analysis (ICA) were introduced to establish an SOH model of battery modules against experimental charge and discharge voltage data in the process of capacity calibration. As a new SOH evaluation algorithm, the discrete Fréchet distance was also proposed for the battery module, and the concept of mean Fréchet distance (MFD) was given as a quick evaluation index of the module’s SOH. The results showed that there was a fine negative linear dependence of module SOH on MFD. The discrete Fréchet distance method has better accuracy than ICA and PDF in terms of module SOH evaluation.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Y. Yang, E. G. Okonkwo, G. Huang, S. Xu, W. Sun and Y. He, Energy Storage Mater., 36 (2021) 186.
Y. Zhang, Y. Li, Y. Tao, J. Ye, A. Pan, X. Li, Q. Liao and Z. Wang, Energy, 193 (2020) 116555.
